serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Agliata di Gamberi is approximately 400 g, comprising sautéed tiger prawns in spicy cherry tomato sauce accompanied by two to three slices of homemade garlic bread.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Shrimp, mixed species, cooked, moist heat (FDC ID 175180) combined with component estimates for garlic bread and tomato sauce
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Agliata di Gamberi exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC cooked shrimp (FDC ID 175180) as the dominant protein component, blended with USDA FDC estimates for garlic bread (FDC ID 172023) and cherry tomato sauce components, weighted to reflect the dish composition (approximately 50% prawns, 30% garlic bread, 20% tomato-garlic sauce). Input per_100g values from the source record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA-derived composite and retained where consistent. Energy 162.5 kcal, protein 8.75 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 10.0 g are plausible for this composite. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate using mixed seafood dish with bread component.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Agliata di Gamberi. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the dish composition: prawns and tomato sauce components have negligible GI contribution; the primary carbohydrate source is garlic bread (white bread GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database), but garlic bread constitutes approximately 30% of the dish by weight and is consumed with high-protein prawns and fat from olive oil, which substantially attenuates the postprandial glycaemic response. A blended GI of approximately 45 is defensible. Glycemic load per 400 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 40.0) / 100 = 18.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g; a full 400 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, representing 52–60% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 200–250 g, request garlic bread without added butter or salt, and avoid adding condiments at the table; the olive oil and potassium (~220 mg/100 g from prawns and tomatoes) partially offset the sodium load but do not eliminate the concern at full serving size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated GI of 45 (low) and moderate carbohydrate content of 10 g per 100 g; the 400 g serving provides approximately 40 g carbohydrate, primarily from garlic bread (refined starch), which requires portion awareness.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this dish in a balanced meal plan by limiting garlic bread to one slice to reduce refined carbohydrate intake to approximately 20–25 g per serving; the high protein content (8.75 g/100 g) from prawns and the low sugar content (1.25 g/100 g) support glycaemic control, and the olive oil and fibre from tomatoes further moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 75 mg per 100 g (300 mg per full serving) and sodium 300 mg per 100 g are the primary cardiovascular concerns; however, saturated fat is relatively low at 3.0 g/100 g and olive oil provides cardioprotective monounsaturated fatty acids.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume a half-portion (200 g) to keep dietary cholesterol below 150 mg and sodium below 600 mg per meal; the extra-virgin olive oil component contributes oleic acid and polyphenols with established cardioprotective effects, and the prawn protein is lean, but the garlic bread butter content adds saturated fat — requesting garlic bread with olive oil instead of butter is advisable.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Extra-virgin olive oil (oleocanthal), garlic (allicin), and parsley (flavonoids, vitamin C 10 mg/100 g) provide anti-inflammatory phytonutrients; saturated fat is low at 3.0 g/100 g.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory ingredient profile; however, cherry tomatoes and chilli are nightshade vegetables whose solanine content may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals, and the garlic bread butter should be substituted with olive oil to minimise saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Agliata di Gamberi is an Italian-inspired sautéed prawn dish served in Singapore's restaurant and food delivery context. The dish features tiger prawns (Penaeus monodon) sautéed in extra-virgin olive oil with generous amounts of garlic and chilli flakes, finished in a spicy cherry tomato sauce and garnished with fresh parsley. It is accompanied by homemade garlic bread, typically two to three slices of baguette or ciabatta brushed with garlic butter and toasted. The flavour profile is bold and savoury — the sweetness of fresh cherry tomatoes balances the heat of chilli and the pungency of garlic, while the prawns contribute a firm, slightly springy texture and a clean oceanic sweetness. The olive oil provides a rich, fruity mouthfeel. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion delivers approximately 162.5 kcal, 8.75 g protein, 10.0 g fat (predominantly monounsaturated from olive oil), and 10.0 g carbohydrate. Sodium is 300 mg/100 g, making portion control important for hypertensive individuals. Cholesterol is 75 mg/100 g from the prawns. The dish has an estimated glycaemic index of 45 (low), attenuated by the protein and fat co-ingested with the garlic bread carbohydrate. It is suitable for most adults in moderate portions but requires sodium and cholesterol awareness for cardiovascular and hypertensive conditions. The dish is not suitable for crustacean-allergic individuals and contains gluten (garlic bread) and dairy (butter). In Singapore, this dish appears on Italian restaurant menus and GrabFood delivery platforms as a premium seafood starter or light main course.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Agliata di Gamberi exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC cooked shrimp and garlic bread component estimates, cross-checked against the input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7). The composite estimation is methodologically sound but introduces uncertainty, warranting a medium confidence rating. GI is an analogous estimate. Multilingual names are provided for Singapore context per cuisine rules.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central cooked shrimp (FDC ID 175180) and garlic bread components (FDC ID 172023), weighted by approximate dish composition (50% prawns, 30% garlic bread, 20% tomato-garlic sauce). Input ai_generated values were retained where consistent with USDA-derived estimates. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite Italian prawn dish preparation is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of 2026-04-16; individual component data exists but reliable composite weighting for these micronutrients cannot be confirmed without laboratory analysis. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (4.5 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.2 g) are estimated from USDA FDC olive oil and shrimp fatty acid profiles; these are approximate. 4. potassium_mg (220.0 mg) is estimated from USDA FDC shrimp and cherry tomato components. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Agliata di Gamberi was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 6. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was listed as HPB Singapore but does not correspond to the official HPB Foodo nutrient database; it has been replaced with the USDA FoodData Central as the primary approved source. 7. The input ethnic_tags included 'Pasta', 'Pizza', and 'Chicken Dish' which are incorrect for this dish and have been removed. 8. Halal status is unconfirmed as the dish contains prawns (generally halal) but garlic bread may contain non-halal butter depending on preparation; is_halal is set to false pending confirmation.
